reasoning: There are currently no approved vaccines for Norovirus globally. The leading candidate, Moderna's mRNA-1403, entered Phase 3 trials in 2024 but was placed on clinical hold by the FDA in February 2025 due to a case of Guillain-Barré syndrome. Even before this hold, the earliest hope for approval was 2026. Another prominent candidate by HilleVax failed in its Phase 2b trial in 2024, and other candidates like Vaxart are only in early clinical stages. Given that vaccine approvals require extensive regulatory review after the completion of Phase 3 trials—which are currently stalled or years away from finishing—there is virtually no realistic path to approval and subsequent inclusion on the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page by October 2025. The probabilities are kept extremely low in the near term and rise only slightly over time to account for highly unlikely outlier scenarios (such as an emergency approval of a foreign vaccine, or Wikipedia vandalism/editor errors temporarily making the article say "Yes" on a resolution date).
